openmeter/sink/flushhandler/ingestnotifi.purpose: Post-flush bridge that transforms successfully flushed SinkMessages into EventBatchedIngest domain events and publishes them to Watermill's balance-worker Kafka topic, enabling downstream entitlement burn-down recalculation per subject. All logic is encapsulated behind the flushhandler.FlushEventHandler interface returned by NewHandler.

patterns.name: FlushEventHandler wrapping via flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler
patterns.description: The internal handler struct is never returned directly. NewHandler always wraps the OnFlushSuccess callback with flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler using FlushEventHandlerOptions{Name, Callback, Logger, MetricMeter}. Callers receive flushhandler.FlushEventHandler, not *handler.
patterns.example: return flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler(flushhandler.FlushEventHandlerOptions{Name: "ingest_notification", Callback: handler.OnFlushSuccess, Logger: logger, MetricMeter: metricMeter})

patterns.name: HandlerConfig.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: NewHandler calls config.Validate() as its first action and propagates any error to the caller. Never assign config fields before validation. MaxEventsInBatch <= 0 must be rejected.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Subject-level grouping + lo.Uniq merge before publish
patterns.description: OnFlushSuccess groups events by namespace+subjectKey using lo.GroupBy, merges MeterSlugs with lo.Uniq, and chunks RawEvents to MaxEventsInBatch using lo.Chunk. Never publish one Kafka message per raw SinkMessage.
patterns.example: iEventsBySubject := lo.GroupBy(iEvents, func(event ingestevents.EventBatchedIngest) string { return event.Namespace.ID + "/" + event.SubjectKey })

patterns.name: StoredAt set once per flush batch
patterns.description: time.Now() is called once at the top of OnFlushSuccess and reused as StoredAt for all events. Never call time.Now() inside the per-event loop.
patterns.example: now := time.Now() // used as StoredAt: now for all events in the batch

patterns.name: Nil Serialized guard before event construction
patterns.description: SinkMessages are filtered with lo.Filter to exclude those where Serialized == nil before any mapping. Any code path building EventBatchedIngest must apply this guard.
patterns.example: filtered := lo.Filter(events, func(event sinkmodels.SinkMessage, _ int) bool { return event.Serialized != nil })

patterns.name: Publish via eventbus.Publisher only
patterns.description: All publishing goes through h.publisher.Publish(ctx, event) where publisher is eventbus.Publisher. Never import raw Watermill message types directly in this package.
patterns.example: if err := h.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { finalErr = errors.Join(finalErr, err) }

patterns.name: MeterSlugs use meter.Key not meter ID
patterns.description: getMeterSlugsFromMeters extracts meter.Meter.Key (the slug). Open-source downstream consumers have no access to internal IDs; always use Key.

key_file_guides.role: Owns all grouping, merging, chunking, and publishing logic. NewHandler is the sole public constructor returning (flushhandler.FlushEventHandler, error).
key_file_guides.watch_for: MaxEventsInBatch must be validated before use — zero or negative values cause infinite loops in lo.Chunk. StoredAt is approximated as time.Now() after flush completion, not the actual ClickHouse write timestamp. errors.Join accumulates per-event publish errors; OnFlushSuccess continues publishing remaining events even when one fails.

key_file_guides.role: Defines EventBatchedIngest and its marshaler.Event compliance. Exports EventVersionSubsystem so balance-worker consumers can subscribe by subsystem prefix.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeterSlugs must remain slugs not IDs. The var _ marshaler.Event compile-time assertion must be kept when adding new event structs. Event struct must stay a pure value type — no pointers to mutable shared state.

anti_patterns: Publishing one Kafka message per SinkMessage instead of grouping by namespace+subject and merging MeterSlugs with lo.Uniq
Using meter IDs instead of meter.Key (slug) in EventBatchedIngest.MeterSlugs — downstream open-source consumers cannot resolve IDs
Calling time.Now() inside the per-event loop — StoredAt must be set once per flush batch for consistency
Bypassing flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler and returning the internal *handler struct directly as flushhandler.FlushEventHandler
Importing raw Watermill message types directly in this package — always publish through eventbus.Publisher

decisions.decision: Subject-level grouping with lo.GroupBy before publish
decisions.rationale: Balance-worker processes recalculations per subject; batching all meter slugs for a subject into one Kafka message reduces message count and ensures atomicity of balance recalculation triggers per subject.

decisions.decision: MaxEventsInBatch chunking to cap RawEvents per Kafka message
decisions.rationale: Kafka enforces a max message size; unbounded RawEvents accumulation per subject on high-volume traffic would exceed that limit. Chunking keeps each message within safe bounds.

decisions.decision: StoredAt approximated from time.Now() post-flush rather than per-event timestamps
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse write completion time is the semantically correct stored-at for downstream balance recalculation. A single now per batch is a fair approximation and avoids per-row timestamp tracking overhead.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new FlushEventHandler in this package — full NewHandler signature pattern
